annotations.label: Krull dimension

anchor.snippet: the supremum of the lengths of all chains of prime ideals

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim R` is defined as `Order.krullDim (PrimeSpectrum R)`, the supremum of lengths of chains of primes.

annotations.label: Krull dimension can be infinite for Noetherian rings

anchor.snippet: The Krull dimension need not be finite even for a Noetherian ring

annotations.note: Nagata's example of an infinite-dimensional Noetherian ring is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dimension of an affine variety

anchor.snippet: the dimension of the affine variety defined by an ideal

annotations.note: Mathlib has `ringKrullDim` and topological/spectrum dimension, but no dedicated classical affine-variety dimension matching V(I).

annotations.label: Field has Krull dimension 0

anchor.snippet: A field k has Krull dimension 0

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_field` states the Krull dimension of any field is 0.

annotations.label: Local ring of dimension zero

anchor.snippet: A local ring has Krull dimension 0 if and only if every element of its maximal ideal is nilpotent

annotations.note: This TFAE equates `KrullDimLE 0 ∧ IsLocalRing` with `∀ x, IsNilpotent x ↔ ¬IsUnit x` (i.e. every maximal-ideal element is nilpotent).

annotations.label: Length of a chain of prime ideals

anchor.snippet: the length is the number of strict inclusions, not the number of primes

annotations.note: `LTSeries`/`RelSeries.length` counts the number of `<`-steps (strict inclusions), one less than the number of terms.

annotations.label: Krull dimension (formal)

anchor.snippet: to be the supremum of the lengths of all chains of prime ideals in

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim` is the supremum over `LTSeries (PrimeSpectrum R)` of their lengths.

annotations.label: Height of a prime ideal

anchor.snippet: to be the supremum of the lengths of all chains of prime ideals contained in

annotations.note: `Ideal.primeHeight` is defined as `Order.height` of the prime in `PrimeSpectrum R`, the sup of lengths of descending prime chains below it.

annotations.label: Height zero iff minimal prime

anchor.snippet: A prime ideal has height zero if and only if it is a minimal prime ideal

annotations.note: `Ideal.primeHeight_eq_zero_iff` states `primeHeight I = 0 ↔ I ∈ minimalPrimes R`.

annotations.label: Krull dimension as supremum of heights

anchor.snippet: The Krull dimension of a ring is the supremum of the heights of all maximal ideals

annotations.note: `Ideal.sup_primeHeight_of_maximal_eq_ringKrullDim` shows the supremum of prime heights of maximal ideals equals `ringKrullDim R`.

annotations.label: Prime ideals have finite height in Noetherian rings

anchor.snippet: every prime ideal has finite height

annotations.note: `Ideal.finiteHeight_of_isNoetherianRing` gives every ideal (hence every prime) in a Noetherian ring finite height.

annotations.label: Noetherian ring of infinite Krull dimension (Nagata)

anchor.snippet: Nagata gave an example of a Noetherian ring of infinite Krull dimension

annotations.note: Nagata's infinite-dimensional Noetherian ring example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Catenary ring

anchor.snippet: A ring is called catenary if any inclusion

annotations.note: There is no catenary-ring definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Universally catenary ring

anchor.snippet: A ring is called universally catenary if any finitely generated algebra over it is catenary

annotations.note: Universally catenary rings are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-catenary Noetherian ring (Nagata)

anchor.snippet: Nagata gave an example of a Noetherian ring which is not catenary

annotations.note: No catenary notion and no such Nagata example exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Krull's height theorem

anchor.snippet: if and only if it is a minimal prime ideal over an ideal generated by

annotations.note: Krull's height theorem (`height_le_iff_exists_minimalPrimes`, plus `height_le_spanRank_toENat_of_mem_minimalPrimes`) gives height ≤ n iff minimal over an n-generated ideal.

annotations.label: Height of an ideal

anchor.snippet: is the infimum of the heights of all prime ideals containing

annotations.note: `Ideal.height` is the infimum of the prime heights of the minimal primes over the ideal.

annotations.label: Krull dimension equals dimension of the spectrum

anchor.snippet: the Krull dimension of R is equal to the dimension of its spectrum as a topological space

annotations.note: `PrimeSpectrum.topologicalKrullDim_eq_ringKrullDim` equates the topological Krull dimension of `PrimeSpectrum R` with `ringKrullDim R`.

annotations.label: Dimension of a polynomial ring over a field

anchor.snippet: The dimension of a polynomial ring over a field

annotations.note: `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing` gives dim = dim R + n, specializing (R a field, dim 0) to dim k[x₁,…,xₙ] = n.

annotations.label: Dimension of R[x] for Noetherian R

anchor.snippet: if R is a Noetherian ring of dimension n , then the dimension of R [ x ] is n + 1

annotations.note: `Polynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing` states `ringKrullDim R[X] = ringKrullDim R + 1` for Noetherian R.

annotations.label: Dimension of R[x] for non-Noetherian R

anchor.snippet: If the Noetherian hypothesis is dropped, then R [ x ] can have dimension anywhere between n + 1 and 2 n + 1

annotations.note: The bounds n+1 ≤ dim R[X] ≤ 2n+1 exist (`ringKrullDim_le` and `ringKrullDim_succ_le_ringKrullDim_polynomial`), but the claim that intermediate values are attained is not formalized.

annotations.label: An ideal of height 2

anchor.snippet: has height 2 since we can form the maximal ascending chain of prime ideals

annotations.note: This specific height-2 computation in a polynomial ring is not a theorem in Mathlib, though `Ideal.height` infrastructure exists.

annotations.label: Height of an irreducible-polynomial ideal

anchor.snippet: we can easily compute the height since the smallest prime ideal containing

annotations.note: This worked example computing the height of an irreducible polynomial's ideal is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ring of integers has dimension 1

anchor.snippet: The ring of integers Z has dimension 1

annotations.note: ℤ is a PID and not a field, so `IsPrincipalIdealRing.ringKrullDim_eq_one` gives `ringKrullDim ℤ = 1` (no ℤ-specific lemma).

annotations.label: PID that is not a field has dimension 1

anchor.snippet: any principal ideal domain that is not a field has dimension 1

annotations.note: `IsPrincipalIdealRing.ringKrullDim_eq_one` proves a PID domain that is not a field has `ringKrullDim = 1`.

annotations.label: Integral domain is a field iff dimension zero

anchor.snippet: An integral domain is a field if and only if its Krull dimension is zero

annotations.note: `Ring.KrullDimLE.isField_of_isDomain` (dim 0 → field) together with `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField` (field → dim 0) give the equivalence for domains.

annotations.label: Dedekind domains have dimension one

anchor.snippet: Dedekind domains that are not fields

annotations.note: Dedekind domains are defined via `Ring.DimensionLEOne` (dim ≤ 1); the exact `ringKrullDim = 1` is only proven for DVRs/PIDs, not Dedekind domains generally.

annotations.label: Krull dimension of the zero ring

anchor.snippet: The Krull dimension of the zero ring is typically defined to be either

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim_eq_bot_of_subsingleton` sets the trivial ring's dimension to ⊥ (−∞), one of the conventional choices.

annotations.label: Zero ring is the only ring with negative dimension

anchor.snippet: The zero ring is the only ring with a negative dimension

annotations.note: `ringKrullDim_nonneg_of_nontrivial` (nontrivial ⇒ ≥ 0) with `ringKrullDim_eq_bot_of_subsingleton` shows only the trivial ring has negative dimension.

annotations.label: Artinian iff Noetherian and dimension at most zero

anchor.snippet: A ring is Artinian if and only if it is Noetherian and its Krull dimension is

annotations.note: `isArtinianRing_iff_isNoetherianRing_krullDimLE_zero` states `IsArtinianRing R ↔ IsNoetherianRing R ∧ Ring.KrullDimLE 0 R`.

annotations.label: Integral extension preserves dimension

anchor.snippet: An integral extension of a ring has the same dimension as the ring does

annotations.note: No theorem equating Krull dimension across an integral ring extension was found (only going-up/going-down infrastructure).

annotations.label: Krull dimension bounded by transcendence degree

anchor.snippet: the Krull dimension of R is less than or equal to the transcendence degree of the field of fractions of R over k

annotations.note: No result relating Krull dimension to transcendence degree of the fraction field exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equality for finitely generated algebras

anchor.snippet: The equality holds if R is finitely generated as an algebra

annotations.note: The dimension = transcendence degree equality for finitely generated algebras is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dimension via associated graded ring

anchor.snippet: is the supremum of the heights of maximal ideals of R containing I

annotations.note: Sup-of-heights-of-maximal-ideals characterizations exist, but associated-graded-ring dimension theory and the I-relative statement are not formalized.

annotations.label: Noetherian ring of dimension zero is a product of local rings

anchor.snippet: A commutative Noetherian ring of Krull dimension zero is a direct product of a finite number

annotations.note: Mathlib has the decomposition of a quotient by a nilradical power into a product of local rings (and reduced Artinian = product of fields via `equivPi`), but not a single clean `R ≅ ∏ local` statement.

annotations.label: Cohen–Macaulay ring

anchor.snippet: A Noetherian local ring is called a Cohen–Macaulay ring if its dimension is equal to its depth

annotations.note: Cohen–Macaulay rings are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular local ring is Cohen–Macaulay

anchor.snippet: A regular local ring is an example of such a ring

annotations.note: Without a Cohen–Macaulay definition, the statement that regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay is not formalized.

annotations.label: UFD iff height 1 primes are principal

anchor.snippet: A Noetherian integral domain is a unique factorization domain if and only if every height 1 prime ideal is principal

annotations.note: Kaplansky's criterion (UFD iff every nonzero prime contains a prime element) is formalized, but the specific height-1-prime-principal characterization is not.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for reduced dimension-zero rings

anchor.snippet: the three following conditions are equivalent

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a reduced Artinian ring is a finite product of fields, but the full TFAE for reduced dimension-zero rings (incl. von Neumann regularity) is not assembled.

annotations.label: Krull dimension of a module

anchor.snippet: to be the Krull dimension of the quotient of

annotations.note: `Module.supportDim` (dim of the support) equals `ringKrullDim (R ⧸ annihilator R M)` for finite modules (`supportDim_eq_ringKrullDim_quotient_annihilator`).

annotations.label: Krull dimension as deviation of poset of submodules

anchor.snippet: is defined as the deviation of the poset of submodules ordered by inclusion

annotations.note: The deviation-of-a-poset definition of (noncommutative) Krull dimension is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Agreement of definitions for Noetherian rings

anchor.snippet: For commutative Noetherian rings, this is the same as the definition using chains of prime ideals

annotations.note: The deviation definition is absent, so its agreement with the prime-chain definition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Definitions differ for non-Noetherian rings

anchor.snippet: The two definitions can be different for commutative rings which are not Noetherian

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the deviation-based dimension, so the comparison with the prime-chain definition is absent.
